title: Replace photo URL inputs with JPEG file upload inputs for property photos

requirements.text: Update the backend Property data model to store photos as base64-encoded JPEG blobs (or stable binary blobs) instead of URL strings. Change the 'photos' field type from [Text] to [Blob] or a record containing filename and blob data. Update createProperty, updateProperty, and getPropertyById to accept and return photo blob data accordingly.

quotes: Remove photo url and add photo with jpeg

acceptanceCriteria: The Property type in main.mo no longer contains a plain URL string array for photos
The photos field accepts JPEG binary/blob data
createProperty and updateProperty backend methods accept photo blobs
getPropertyById returns photo blob data that can be rendered as images on the frontend

requirements.text: In PostPropertyPage (Features & Photos step), remove all photo URL text input fields and replace them with a file upload input that accepts JPEG image files (accept='image/jpeg,image/jpg'). Allow uploading multiple files. Read uploaded files as binary data (e.g., via FileReader as ArrayBuffer or base64) and store them in component state for submission. Display thumbnail previews of selected images before submission.

quotes: Remove photo url and add photo with jpeg

acceptanceCriteria: No photo URL text input fields exist in PostPropertyPage
A file input with accept='image/jpeg,image/jpg' is present in the Features & Photos step
Multiple JPEG files can be selected at once
Thumbnail previews of selected photos are shown before submission
Selected photo data is submitted with the property form data

requirements.text: In EditPropertyPage, remove all photo URL text input fields and replace them with a JPEG file upload input (accept='image/jpeg,image/jpg'). Pre-populate the photo preview area with existing photos from the property data. Allow the user to upload new JPEG files to replace or add to existing photos. Display thumbnail previews of both existing and newly selected photos.

quotes: Remove photo url and add photo with jpeg

acceptanceCriteria: No photo URL text input fields exist in EditPropertyPage
A JPEG file upload input is present
Existing property photos are shown as thumbnails when the edit form loads
New JPEG files can be selected and previewed before saving
Updated photo data is submitted correctly on form save

requirements.text: Update PropertyCard, PropertyListingPage, PropertyDetailPage (ImageGallerySlider), and any other component that renders property photos to handle the new blob/base64 photo format. Convert stored photo blob data to object URLs or base64 data URIs for rendering in tags. Ensure backward compatibility where no photos exist (show a placeholder image).

quotes: Remove photo url and add photo with jpeg

acceptanceCriteria: PropertyCard renders uploaded JPEG photos correctly using blob/base64 data
ImageGallerySlider on PropertyDetailPage renders all uploaded JPEG photos
No broken image icons appear for properties with uploaded photos
Properties without any photos show a fallback placeholder image

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor in backend/main.mo
Only generate backend/migration.mo if the backend state schema upgrade requires data migration
nonGoals: Supporting image formats other than JPEG/JPG
Cloud storage or CDN integration for photos
Changing any other property fields beyond the photos field
